title: Poached Mexican Tortillas Tacos
description: A light and flavorful dish featuring poached Mexican tortillas, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This kosher-friendly recipe is made with individual mini cocottes for a unique presentation.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cut the corn tortillas into quarters and set aside.
In a large bowl, combine the chicken broth, water, and lime juice.
Bring the broth mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer.
Add the diced onion and bell pepper to the broth and cook until tender.
Add the tortilla quarters to the broth and poach for 2-3 minutes.
Using a slotted spoon, remove the poached tortillas from the broth and place them in the individual mini cocottes.
Drizzle the olive oil over the tortillas and top with shredded cheese, chopped cilantro, salt, and pepper.
Place the mini cocottes in the oven and bake for 5-7 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the mini cocottes from the oven and serve immediately.
Cook poultry to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F before serving.
